sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Casa Pia, currently sitting at 8th in the league with 42 points, hosts Estoril Praia, who are just one spot below in 9th also with 42 points, in Round 31 at Estadio Pina Manique in Lisbon. This closely contested matchup promises to be a tight battle as both teams are level on points. The referee for the game, Goncalo Neves, is known for averaging 5.1 yellow cards per game, which could influence the flow of this encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between Casa Pia and Estoril Praia, the results have been quite balanced with 1 win for Casa Pia, 2 draws, and 2 wins for Estoril Praia. Their most recent encounter on December 15, 2024, saw Casa Pia triumph 2-0 away at Estoril Praia, with Patrick Sequeira earning Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 7.9. Head-to-head trends also show that 5 of the last 7 games had less than 2.5 goals, suggesting low-scoring affairs, while 5 of the last 6 matches saw more than 4.5 cards, hinting at a physical contest.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Casa Pia
sections.text: For Casa Pia, forward Cassiano stands out as a major threat with 10 goals this season, placing him 5th in the league's top scorers list, and overperforming his expected goals (xG) of 8.56 by 1.444. Midfielder Leonardo Lelo has also been influential with 4 assists and an average Sofascore rating of 7.08, one of the highest in the squad. Another name to watch is Gaizka Larrazabal, who has contributed 1 goal and 3 assists, adding creativity from midfield.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Estoril Praia
sections.text: Estoril Praia boasts firepower up front with Alejandro Marqués, who has also scored 10 goals this season, ranking 5th in the league alongside Cassiano, and overperforming his xG of 7.27 by 2.7291. João Carvalho, a standout in past encounters with an average Sofascore rating of 7.375 across 4 starts against Casa Pia, has netted 6 goals this season with a remarkable xG overperformance of 4.2541. Additionally, midfielder Jordan Holsgrove impresses with an average rating of 7.15 and 3 assists, offering playmaking ability.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Statistics
sections.text: Casa Pia have struggled recently, with no wins in their last 3 matches and a tendency to concede first in 6 of their last 7 games. They’ve scored 36 goals this season (9th in the league) but underperformed their expected goals by 4.5211. Estoril Praia, on the other hand, have seen over 2.5 goals in 8 of their last 10 matches, indicating an attacking approach. Both teams average under 10.5 corners in most recent games, with Casa Pia at 3.9375 corners per game (12th in the league).

sections.subtitle: Scoring Patterns and Timing
sections.text: Casa Pia tend to score heavily between the 31st and 45th minutes, with 10 goals (24.39% of their total) in this period, and also late in games between the 76th and 90th minutes with 6 goals (24.39%). Estoril Praia, conversely, are most dangerous in the final 15 minutes, scoring 11 goals (21.57% of their total) between the 76th and 90th minutes. This suggests both teams could push for late drama in this clash.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Physical Play
sections.text: Discipline could play a significant role in this match as Casa Pia have committed 470 fouls this season (5th most in the league) and received 84 yellow cards (2.625 per game). Recent trends show 5 of their last 7 games having more than 4.5 cards. With referee Goncalo Neves averaging 5.1 yellows and 0.57 red cards per game, expect a match that could see frequent stoppages and cautions.
